steps.title: Focus on significance
steps.content: The student wants the significance of a similarity between two kits, not just any shared data point. The notes say Pbact was most effective because it alone combined sufficient yield with purity near the ideal, making the meaningful similarity that the other two kits fell short.

steps.title: Avoid trivial similarities
steps.content: Choice A states a true but weak overlap that does not explain effectiveness. Choice C falsely says CTAB 2X and Pbact were equal in purity. Choice D wrongly says Pbact had insufficient yield and does not focus on CTAB 2X and Pplant. The choice about CTAB 2X and Pplant being less effective captures the significant shared status. The answer is B.
